items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Grange: Memphis didn’t deserve LeBron James’ criticism
items.blurb: Grange: Memphis didn’t deserve LeBron James’ criticism — LeBron James’ harsh words about Memphis were a tough pill to swallow for Grizzlies fans. It may be tough for Memphis to thrive in today’s NBA, but no one deserves to hear criticism like that.

items.headline: Latest Luka Doncic injury update may have big implications for Lakers star
items.blurb: Latest Luka Doncic injury update may have big implications for Lakers star — The hamstring injury suffered by Los Angeles Lakers superstar Luka Doncic on Thursday night against the Oklahoma City Thunder is severe enough that it could keep him out for an extended period of time. The initial timetable is not exactly encouraging for the Lakers, or for his chances of winning major NBA awards and honor

items.headline: Cooper Flagg's historic scoring night can't get Mavericks a win
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg's historic scoring night can't get Mavericks a win — Cooper Flagg became the first teenager to score 50 points in an NBA game Friday. That wasn't enough to get the Dallas Mavericks a win.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: 76ers knock off Timberwolves as Embiid returns
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: 76ers knock off Timberwolves as Embiid returns

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Magic 138, Mavericks 127
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Magic 138, Mavericks 127 — Wendell Carter posted 28 points on 50 per cent shooting, while Desmond Bane added 27 points and three steals as the Orlando Magic earned a 138-127 win over the Dallas Mavericks. Cooper Flagg tallied a new career-high with 51 points in the loss.

items.headline: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg becomes first teen to score 50 points
items.blurb: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg becomes first teen to score 50 points

items.headline: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg was bound to make NBA history
items.blurb: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg was bound to make NBA history — Even during another loss for the Dallas Mavericks, rookie Cooper Flagg continued to make his debut season historic. The No. 1 overall pick from the 2025 NBA Draft put together a performance the league had never seen before.

items.headline: How Raptors’ Barrett backed up talk with dominant performance vs. Grizzlies
items.blurb: How Raptors’ Barrett backed up talk with dominant performance vs. Grizzlies — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton, and Matt Bonner break down the Toronto Raptors’ blowout win against the Memphis Grizzlies and explain how forward RJ Barrett was able to back up his talk with a dominant performance on the court.

items.headline: Raptors coast past Grizzlies to snap two-game skid
items.blurb: Raptors coast past Grizzlies to snap two-game skid — RJ Barrett scored 25 points, Brandon Ingram had 17 points and seven rebounds and the Toronto Raptors built a first-half lead, extended it in the third and coasted to a 128-96 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies on Friday night.

items.headline: ‘We can beat anybody’: Raptors’ Barrett confident ahead of Celtics matchup
items.blurb: ‘We can beat anybody’: Raptors’ Barrett confident ahead of Celtics matchup — RJ Barrett spoke to the media following the Toronto Raptors decisive win over the Memphis Grizzlies, saying they kept their foot on the gas from tip-off to final buzzer, adding that he’s eager to face the Boston Celtics in the team’s next matchup.

items.headline: NBA Investigating Bucks Over Giannis Antetokounmpo Availability Dispute
items.blurb: NBA Investigating Bucks Over Giannis Antetokounmpo Availability Dispute — The NBA is investigating the Milwaukee Bucks over their handling of the player participation policy and potentially inconsistent statements regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo's health, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania on Friday. The league has already interviewed Antetokounmpo, the Bucks organization and team doctors. At the

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Raptors 128, Grizzlies 96
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Raptors 128, Grizzlies 96 — R.J. Barrett led the way with a team-high 25 points and Collin Murray-Boyles dropped 19 points off the bench to help the Toronto Raptors beat the Memphis Grizzlies 128-96.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Celtics 133, Bucks 101
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Celtics 133, Bucks 101 — Jaylen Brown racked up a game-high 26 points, while Jayson Tatum added 23 points, 11 rebounds and nine assists as the Boston Celtics earned a commanding 133-101 win over the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Rockets 140, Jazz 106
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Rockets 140, Jazz 106 — Kevin Durant posted 25 points, six assists and two blocks, while Amen Thompson chipped in with 21 points, eight rebounds and two steals as the Houston Rockets cruised to a 140-106 win over the Utah Jazz.

items.headline: South Carolina Ends UConn's Perfect Season, Auriemma-Staley Exchange Caps Heated Game
items.blurb: South Carolina Ends UConn's Perfect Season, Auriemma-Staley Exchange Caps Heated Game — South Carolina exacted revenge on UConn on Friday, stunning the undefeated Huskies 62-48 in the Women's Final Four in Phoenix to advance to its third straight national championship game. The victory was punctuated by a heated postgame confrontation between coaches Geno Auriemma and Dawn Staley, after which Auri

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Knicks 136, Bulls 96
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Knicks 136, Bulls 96 — OG Anunoby led his team with 31 points and eight rebounds as the New York Knicks scored 78 first-half points on their way to a 136-96 victory over the Chicago Bulls.

items.headline: Report: NBA investigating Bucks for handling health of Giannis Antetokounmpo
items.blurb: Report: NBA investigating Bucks for handling health of Giannis Antetokounmpo

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hawks 141, Nets 107
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hawks 141, Nets 107 — CJ McCollum with 25 points and Nickeil Alexander-Walker with 21 points led a balanced Atlanta Hawks’ attack that had seven players finish in double-figures in their 141-107 win over the Brooklyn Nets.

items.headline: NBA investigating Bucks as Giannis Antetokounmpo insists he's healthy
items.blurb: NBA investigating Bucks as Giannis Antetokounmpo insists he's healthy — The NBA is investigating the Bucks for potentially violating the player participation policy as well as possible inconsistent statements regarding the health status of superstar forward Giannis Antetokounmpo.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: 76ers 115, Timberwolves 103
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: 76ers 115, Timberwolves 103 — Paul George scored 23 points, Tyrese Maxey scored 21 points, and Joel Embiid registered 19 points, 13 rebounds, and seven assists as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 115-103.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hornets 129, Pacers 108
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hornets 129, Pacers 108 — Brandon Miller scored 22 points, Kon Knueppel scored 20 points, and LaMelo Ball posted 18 points, nine assists, and six rebounds as the Charlotte Hornets scored 38 first quarter points on their way to a 129-108 victory over the Indiana Pacers.

items.headline: USC's JuJu Watkins Back On Court At USA Basketball Camp, Expected Ready For Next Season
items.blurb: USC's JuJu Watkins Back On Court At USA Basketball Camp, Expected Ready For Next Season — JuJu Watkins is making steady progress in her return from a torn right ACL, shooting on the practice court at the Phoenix Mercury facility during USA Basketball national team training camp without a knee brace. The Southern California star, who lost her entire 2025-26 season to the injury, expects to be ready

items.headline: UConn's Sarah Strong Named AP Women's Basketball Player Of The Year
items.blurb: UConn's Sarah Strong Named AP Women's Basketball Player Of The Year — Sarah Strong was named the Associated Press women's basketball Player of the Year Thursday, becoming just the fifth sophomore in history to claim the honor after guiding UConn to an undefeated 38-0 record and a Final Four berth. Strong received 25 of 31 votes from the national media panel, with Vanderbilt's Mikayla Blakes earnin

items.headline: Massive dunk from Ingram called an offensive foul after replay review
items.blurb: Massive dunk from Ingram called an offensive foul after replay review — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram gets a pass from Jamal Shead and drives to the basket for a monstrous poster slam on the Memphis Grizzlies’ Dariq Whitehead that was initially called a defensive foul but was overturned to a foul on Ingram.

items.headline: Raptors’ Rajakovic defends Memphis after LeBron James’ criticism
items.blurb: Raptors’ Rajakovic defends Memphis after LeBron James’ criticism — A pair of NBA head coaches defended Memphis and its culture Friday following criticism by Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James that the Grizzlies should just relocate to Nashville.

items.blurb: Luka Doncic to file rare NBA grievance to protect MVP eligibility after injury — Doncic was ruled out for the remainder of the regular season with no confirmed timeline for his return.

items.blurb: Final Four Predictions: Who Wins Saturday and Who Reaches the National Championship Game? — The Final Four is finally here, and this year’s field feels less like a bracket surprise and more like a collision of heavyweights. items.headline: Kristaps Porzingis Keeping Options Open In Free Agency
items.blurb: Kristaps Porzingis Keeping Options Open In Free Agency — Kristaps Porzingis is keeping his options open this offseason despite finding comfort with the Golden State Warriors, declining to commit to a return when asked directly about his future with the franchise. "It's hard to say," Porzingis told The Athletic after Thursday's 118-111 loss to Cleveland. "Of course, it would be nice for me to go ah

items.headline: Luka Doncic’s Regular Season Ends at the Worst Possible Time for the Lakers
items.blurb: Luka Doncic’s Regular Season Ends at the Worst Possible Time for the Lakers — The Los Angeles Lakers have been dealt a major blow, as Luka Doncic will miss the remainder of the regular season with a left hamstring strain. items.headline: Luka Doncic Out For Regular Season With Grade 2 Hamstring Strain, Playoff Status Uncertain
items.blurb: Luka Doncic Out For Regular Season With Grade 2 Hamstring Strain, Playoff Status Uncertain — Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic will miss the remainder of the regular season after an MRI Friday revealed a Grade 2 left hamstring strain. Doncic suffered the injury in the third quarter of Thursday's 139-96 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Dallas. A Grade 2 strain typically requires approximately

items.blurb: Luka Doncic to challenge NBA rule in order to qualify for awards under rare exception — Doncic has played in 64 games this season, and he is one game shy of meeting the 65-game minimum requirement.

items.headline: Warriors receive massive Stephen Curry injury update before Rockets game
items.blurb: Warriors receive massive Stephen Curry injury update before Rockets game — The Golden State Warriors lost Stephen Curry on Jan. 30 against the Detroit Pistons after right knee pain and swelling forced him out. The absence stretched into something much longer than anyone expected.

items.headline: Bam Adebayo reveals how 83-point game changed NBA's perception of him
items.blurb: Bam Adebayo reveals how 83-point game changed NBA's perception of him — Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo recently reflected on his historic 83-point performance, asserting that the outburst has fundamentally altered how the league perceives his offensive capabilities.

items.headline: Cameron Boozer Becomes Fifth Freshman To Win AP Player Of The Year
items.blurb: Cameron Boozer Becomes Fifth Freshman To Win AP Player Of The Year — Cameron Boozer has been named the Associated Press men's college basketball Player of the Year , becoming only the fifth freshman to win the award and the second straight Duke player to claim the honor following Cooper Flagg last season. items.headline: A'ja Wilson Confirms Return To Aces On 600 Percent Raise Supermax
items.blurb: A'ja Wilson Confirms Return To Aces On 600 Percent Raise Supermax — A'ja Wilson confirmed Friday she will return to the Las Vegas Aces for the 2026 WNBA season, ending any speculation about the league's best player testing free agency. Wilson made the announcement at USA Basketball training camp. "I love Vegas, I'm not leaving Vegas," Wilson said. "I'm not looking anywhere, I'm looking to win anot

items.headline: Mac McClung Wins G League MVP For 2nd Time In Career
items.blurb: Mac McClung Wins G League MVP For 2nd Time In Career — Mac McClung has been named G League Most Valuable Player for the second time in his career. McClung previously won the award in 2024. McClung averaged a league-high 31.8 points, 7.9 assists and 3.6 rebounds, while shooting 51.5 percent from the field and 38.1 percent from three-point range in 40 games for the Windy City Bulls. items.blurb: Jason Kidd Refutes Mark Cuban's Claims On Luka Doncic Trade, Says He Was 'Informed At 11th Hour' — Dallas Mavericks coach Jason Kidd directly disputed minority owner Mark Cuban's assertion that he played a role in the February 2025 trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers, stating Friday he was kept out of the process entirely until moments before the deal became public. items.headline: Nebraska's Fred Hoiberg Named AP Men's Basketball Coach Of The Year
items.blurb: Nebraska's Fred Hoiberg Named AP Men's Basketball Coach Of The Year — Fred Hoiberg was named the Associated Press men's basketball coach of the year Friday after guiding Nebraska to a 28-win season, the program's first NCAA Tournament victory and a Sweet 16 appearance. items.headline: Jason Kidd doubles down after Mark Cuban's comments on Luka Doncic trade
items.blurb: Jason Kidd doubles down after Mark Cuban's comments on Luka Doncic trade — The fallout from the Luka Doncic-Anthony Davis trade with the Los Angeles Lakers and Dallas Mavericks in 2025 is still happening.

items.blurb: UConn's Coach Dan Hurley Won't Shy Away From Sideline Behavior — Dan Hurley believes the backlash about his on-court behavior is fair. Just over 24 hours before UConn's matchup against Illinois in the Final Four, Hurley said he is not looking for any sympathy regarding the perception of his coaching style. "I'm not a victim.
